title: SQLite/FTS5 Index Builder with Validation Pipeline Pattern
summary: A complete SQLite/FTS5 index builder pipeline: load JSONL chunks → validate (required fields, enums, secret detection, source-code density check, duplicate IDs) → optionally backup existing DB → create schema with FTS5 virtual table → upsert chunks in transaction → write structured report. The pipeline separates validation (--dry-run) from writing (--write), enforcing a two-phase workflow that catches errors before touching the production database. Secret detection uses regex patterns with an allow-list for placeholder/example context.
why_reusable: Any project that builds searchable knowledge bases from structured content needs a reliable index builder. The validation-first, dry-run-before-write pattern prevents corrupt indexes. The secret/source-code detection is directly reusable for any content ingestion pipeline.
adapter_target: Generic content indexing pipeline — extract the load→validate→backup→write→report pattern into a reusable pipeline class with configurable validators.
implementation_notes: Two-phase workflow: --dry-run validates and reports without touching the DB; --write requires --db and --backup flags
Validation checks: required fields, enum values, empty content, secret-like patterns, source-code density > 12 lines as content
Secret detection uses regex for common patterns (AWS keys, OpenAI keys, GitHub tokens, private keys) with an allow-list for placeholder text
Backup is mandatory before write — creates timestamped .bak file
Upsert uses INSERT ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE for idempotent rebuilds
FTS5 content table is cleaned and re-populated per chunk on upsert
Schema creation is idempotent (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS)
tests_required: Unit test: chunk validation catches missing required fields
Unit test: secret detection flags real secrets but allows placeholders
Unit test: source-code density check rejects code-heavy chunks
Integration test: --dry-run produces valid report without writing
Integration test: --write with --backup creates DB and backup file
Integration test: --write without --backup fails with error
risks: Secret detection regex is pattern-based and may miss novel secret formats
Source-code density threshold (12 lines) may need tuning per domain
FTS5 may not be available on all SQLite builds; fallback to LIKE-only must be handled
Backup file naming uses timestamp — concurrent writes within same second may collide
do_not_do: Do not skip --dry-run before --write on production data
Do not write without backup — the --write flag enforces this
Do not rely solely on regex for secret detection in security-critical pipelines
Do not embed the index builder in the API server — it's a build-time tool, not runtime
packaging_impact: Zero — pure Python stdlib. FTS5 is included in standard Python SQLite builds on Windows.
notes: Extracted from DataBase scripts/backend/build_backend_index.py (356 lines). The secret detection regex and source-code density check are directly reusable in any content ingestion pipeline.
